text.title: choreography-vs-orchestration-decision Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the choreography-vs-orchestration-decision methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply only to a single workflow. Never batch unrelated workflows into one ADR.
rule.rationale: Each workflow's saga gets its own ADR for traceability.

rule.statement: Every input field has a declared type and source. Free-form prose without citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed + sourced inputs make the decision falsifiable.

rule.statement: Output names a single accountable owner. No 'team' or 'we'.
Output carries semver version + last_reviewed; stale ADRs flagged on read.
rule.rationale: ADRs decay; review cadence forced by metadata.

rule.statement: Choreography is preferred only when workflow ownership IS naturally distributed; otherwise default to orchestration to centralise debugging cost.
rule.rationale: Most teams underestimate the debug cost of distributed sagas.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
